hymns.title: What shall I do with Jesus?
White pages before us, thank God this is so
In the land of strangers
Drifting away from the Saviour
Some one will enter the pearly gate
There was One who was willing to die in my stead
Come, sinners, to the Living One
Jesus is tenderly calling thee home
O tender and sweet was the Father's voice
Like wand'ring sheep o'er mountains cold
There are two ways for trav'lers, only two ways
"Whosoever heareth," shout, shout the sound!
"Yet there is room!"
Sound the alarm!  let the watchman cry
In darkness I lingered 'mid doubt and despair
O heart bowed down with sorrow!
I've wandered far away from God
Just one touch as He moves along
One thing I of the Lord desire
Christ is knocking at my sad heart
There's a Stranger at the door
If you could see Christ standing here tonight
I cannot drive the nails again
Sing it o'er and o'er again
If you are tired of the load of your sin
A ruler once came to Jesus by night
Would you be free from your burden of sin?
Blessed be the Fountain of blood
Ring the bells of heaven!  there is joy today
Able to deliver!  sound it far and near
O hear my cry, be gracious now to me
We'll build on the Rock, the living Rock
We are building in sorrow or joy
We tell it as we journey t'ward the mansions built above
Knocking, knocking, who is there?
'Twas in the days of careless youth, when life was fair and bright
"Eternity!  where?"  It floats in the air
The day is dead, and Egypt's night returning
In the silent midnight watches
Tell me the story of Jesus
O who is this that cometh
When Jesus shall gather the nations
Called to the feast by the King are we
There's room for you to anchor
While Jesus whispers to you
Jesus, the loving Shepherd
Not in the hour of death
At the feast of Belshazzar and a thousand of his lords
When the Judge shall weigh our motives
Oh, the best friend to have is Jesus
All to Jesus I surrender
What means this eager, anxious throng
I have a Savior, he's pleading in glory
Are you tenting on the lowlands
Hear the words our Saviour hath spoken
Sowing the seed by the daylight fair
There's not a Friend like the lowly Jesus
Would you live for Jesus, and be always pure and good?
Who is on the Lord's side?
Only a step to Jesus!
There were ninety and nine that safely lay
What will you do with the King called Jesus?
My blessed Lord was crucified
Lord Jesus, I long to be perfectly whole
There's life in a look at the sacred cross
Yield not to temptation
Dare to do right, dare to be true!
I think when I read that sweet story of old
Why labor for treasures that rust and decay
Lead them, my God, to Thee
I will early seek the Saviour
Who'll be the next to follow Jesus?
Now just a word for Jesus
Sing them over again to me
Jesus, my Saviour, to Bethlehem came
Softly and tenderly Jesus is calling
Like as a father, pities his child
We have heard a joyful sound
Tho' your sins be as scarlet
Look upon Jesus, sinless is He
While we pray and while we plead
Behold, behold the Lamb of God
By faith I view my Saviour dying
The mistakes of my life are many
There is no love like the love of Jesus
There's a great day coming
Pass me not, O gentle Saviour
Have you been to Jesus for the cleansing power?
Almost persuaded now to believe
I stood outside the gate
There is a gate that stands ajar
Weeping will not save me
What can wash away my sin?
The great Physician now is near
Come, ev'ry soul by sin oppressed
I gave my life for Thee
Not saved are we by trying
Where is my wand'ring boy tonight
Refining fire, go thro' my heart
Crowded is your heart with cares
I hear thy welcome voice
I was once far away from the Saviour
Jesus, my Lord, to thee I cry
Art thou weary, art thou languid
I am trusting Thee, Lord Jesus
Sins of years are washed away
I hear the Saviour say
My faith looks up to thee
I am coming to the cross
O now I see the crimson wave
Jesus, and shall it ever be
Behold the Saviour at the door!
God calling yet!  shall I not hear?
We all, O Lord, have gone astray
The gospel trump is sounding
The sprinkled blood is speaking
O, Comfort to the dreary!
O Jesus!  thou art standing
Forgive, O Lord, the doubts that break
Go not, my soul, in search of Him
Weary of earth and laden with my sin
The Lord first empties whom he fills
Arise, my soul, arise
Come, ye disconsolate, where'er ye languish
Come unto me when shadows darkly gather
Jesus, let thy pitying eye
The great decisive day is at hand
There's a wideness in God's mercy
God is love; His mercy brightens
Sweet the moments, rich in blessing
Nothing but leaves!  The Spirit grieves
Heaven is not far away
Thou art the Way, to thee alone
Baptize us anew
O that the Lord would guide my ways
The last call of mercy now lingers for thee
There is a line by us unseen
The wonders of redeeming love
Chief of sinners tho' I be
Go to dark Gethsemane
Not what these hands have done
Cast out the buyers, Lord
How solemn are the words
He's coming once again
Just as I am, without one plea
There is a fountain fill'd with blood
Flee as a bird to your mountain
I bring my sins to Thee
O spotless Lamb!  I come to thee
Alas!  and did my Savior bleed?
The waters are troubled
Not all the blood of beasts
O where shall rest be found
Depth of mercy! can there be
Come, my soul, thy suit prepare!
'Tis midnight; and on Olives' brow
The day of wrath, that dreadful day
I saw One hanging on the tree
Did Christ o'er sinners weep?
O, for a closer walk with God!
Come, Holy Spirit, come
O, could I find, from day to day
Holy Spirit, light divine
Come, gracious Spirit, heavenly Dove
Come to the living waters, come!
O for that flame of living fire
Return, O wanderer, return
Jesus calls us, o'er the tumult
And must I part with all of self
Take my heart, O Father, take it!
One there is above all others
When I survey the wondrous cross
To-day the Saviour calls
Lord, I hear of show'rs of blessing
There are angels hov'ring round
Come, O thou Traveler unknown
Broad is the road that leads to death
Come, ye sinners, poor and needy
Come to Jesus, Come to Jesus
With tearful eyes I look around
Give, thou, thy youth to God
See, Israel's gentle shepherd stands
Oh, for a heart to praise my God!
Why do we waste on trifling cares
Must Jesus bear the cross alone
Father, I stretch my hands to thee
When, my Savior, shall I be
God shall be first in ev'rything
They bro't their gifts to Jesus
Hallelujahs to Jesus!
To God be the glory, great things He hath done
My heart was distress'd 'neath Jehovah's dread frown
O, spread the tidings round
I will sing of Jesus' love
Would we be joyful in the Lord?
Be silent, be silent
Come out in the sunshine!
There's sunshine in my soul to-day
Redeemed!  how I love to proclaim it!
I can hear my Saviour calling
He will mention them no more forever
Nearer, still nearer, close to Thy heart
I once was in the desert, all weary, sad and lone
Christ has for sin atonement made
Blessed assurance, Jesus is mine!
We'll tarry by the living waters
"There shall be showers of blessing:"
There comes to my heart one sweet strain
The dove of peace sings in my heart
Far away in the depths of my spirit tonight
My soul in sad exile was out on life's sea
Alas!  and did my Saviour bleed
The cross!  it standeth fast
Awake, my soul, and greet the dawn
The name of Jesus is so sweet
Take the name of Jesus with you
Dearest name in earth or heaven
O, the bitter pain and sorrow
Standing on the promises of Christ my King
O worship the Lord in the beauty of holiness
Saved to the uttermost:  I am the Lord's
Safe in the arms of Jesus
Nor silver nor gold hath obtained my redemption
Since Christ my soul from sin set free
Tho' my sins were once like crimson red
Praise Him!  praise Him!  Jesus, our blessed Redeemer!
Wake the song of joy and gladness
O come, let us sing unto the Lord
There is sunlight on the hilltop
Not I but Christ, be honored, loved, exalted
My heart's a tuneful harp when Christ abides within
I feel like singing all the time
Jesus comes with pow'r to gladden
O Jesus my Redeemer
O sing unto the Lord a new song
I have a song I love to sing
I sing the love of God, my Father
One there is above all others
Hear the words of Scripture from the ages past
Oh, bliss of the purified!  bliss of the free!
"Called unto holiness," Church of our God
A beautiful star arose one night
There's a dark and a troubled side of life
"Peace upon earth!" the angels sang
In joyful high and holy lays
I've found a friend in Jesus, He's ev'rything to me
Each cooing dove and sighing bough
Long ago the children sang a song
Do you know what the dewdrops say
Don't forget the Sabbath
I've two little hands to work for Jesus
God sees the little sparrow fall
Little stars that twinkle in the heaven's blue
Jesus, I will follow thee
Sweetly sing, sweetly sing
Our sweetest songs of gladness
All hail the power of Jesus' name
All hail the power of Jesus' name!
All hail the pow'r of Jesus' name!
Praise ye Jehovah's name
Crown him with many crowns
O could I speak the matchless worth
Come, children, hail the Prince of Peace
We are children of a King
Look, ye saints, the sight is glorious
Angels, from the realms of glory
As with gladness men of old
Praise, my soul, the King of Heaven
Angel voices, ever singing
'Tis the blessed hour of prayer, when our hearts lowly bend
Peace!  perfect peace!  in this dark world of sin
List to the chime, 'tis meeting time
Children singing, sweet bells ringing!
Strains of music often greet me
Valley lilies, meek and lowly
I wandered in the shades of night
Brightest and best of the sons of the morning
Jesus, Thou hast promised
God of life and God of light
Welcome!  thrice welcome!
When morning gilds the skies
I am Thine, O Lord, I have heard Thy voice
Jesus loves me!  this I know
Let our hearts be always cheerful
For all the saints who from their labors rest
More like Jesus would I be
Saviour, keep me pure in heart
O for a thousand tongues to sing
Jesus, keep me near the cross
Since on my Saviour I believed
"I know that my Redeemer lives"
I have entered the valley of blessing so sweet
In the blood from the cross
My hope is built on nothing less
O let me walk with thee, my God
Walking with thee, my God
More love to thee, O Christ!
O bliss of the pure ones!
My Jesus, I love thee, I know thou art mine
Down at the cross where my Saviour died
I know I love thee better, Lord
There's no other name like Jesus
Have I need of aught, O Saviour!
God loved the world of sinners lost
Where He may lead me I will go
Fade, fade each earthly joy
When softly falls the twilight hour
Hover o'er me, Holy Spirit
Let the whole Creation cry
All for Jesus, all for Jesus!
Tell me the old, old story
Fairest Lord Jesus
Hark!  the herald angels sing
Thou didst leave Thy throne and Thy kingly crown
Take the world, but give me Jesus
Take my life, and let it be
I lay my sins on Jesus
Live out Thy life within me
O Jesus, I have promised
How dearly God doth love us
O Saviour, precious Saviour
O Word of God incarnate
When, His salvation bringing
Come, praise your Lord and Saviour
There lives a voice within me
To Thee, O dear, dear Saviour!
Holy, holy, holy!  Lord God Almighty!
Worthy, worthy, is the Lamb
The spacious firmament on high
I sing the mighty pow'r of God
My life flows on in endless song
I've found a Friend; oh, such a Friend!
Lord of all being, throned afar
O Love!  how deep, how broad, how high!
O worship the King, all glorious above
O Thou in whose presence my soul takes delight
God's free mercy streameth
Purer yet and purer
Day is dying in the west
Rest of the weary
Lord, thy glory fills the heaven
Hark!  ten thousand harps and voices
Jesus, I my cross have taken
How tedious and tasteless the hours
Love divine, all love excelling
Heav'nly Father, send Thy blessing
Fresh from the throne of glory
O little town of Bethlehem
The Lord in Zion reigneth!
Beneath the cross of Jesus
Praise the Lord, His glories show
Conq'ring kings their titles take
Hark! the herald angels sing
Songs of praise the angels sang
O God, we praise thee, and confess
O Love divine, of all that is
O Thou who art of all that is
I heard a voice, the sweetest voice
I heard the voice of Jesus say
I heard the voice of Jesus say
Majestic sweetness sits enthron'd
A glory in the word we find
A glory gilds the sacred page
How shall the young secure their hearts
Let all the heathen writers join
Joys are flowing like a river
Awake, my soul, to joyful lays
Jesus, my all, to heav'n has gone
Who is on the Lord's side?
Fling out the banner!
God, in the gospel of His Son
Salvation!  O the joyful sound
The heav'ns declare Thy glory, Lord
Again the day awakes in wondrous beauty
For the beauty of the earth
Grant thy blessing, now, O Lord
Praise ye the Father for his loving kindness
Praise ye the Lord; for it is good
Come, ye that love the Lord
Again the day returns of holy rest
Father, again in Jesus' name we meet
Saviour, again to thy dear name we raise
My life, my love, I give to thee
Break Thou the bread of life
The dawn of God's dear Sabbath
O day of rest and gladness
Welcome, delightful morn
Safely thro' another week
It came upon the midnight clear
Come, thou almighty King
Sound, sound the truth abroad!
When all thy mercies, O my God!
O, how happy are they
I would be, dear Saviour, wholly thine
We praise thee, O God, for the Son of thy love
Rejoice and be glad, the Redeemer has come
Come, thou Fount of ev'ry blessing
O happy day!  that fixed my choice
There is no name so sweet on earth
O Love that will not let me go
Thy Word is like a garden, Lord
The King of love my Shepherd is
Stand up, and bless the Lord
By cool Siloam's shady rill
The Lord be with us as we bend
Our Saviour's voice is soft and sweet
O holy Book of truth divine!
I will follow thee, my Saviour
Jesus, thy love can we forget
O Father, I have nought to plead
O Lord, who hidest all our shame
Buried beneath the yielding wave
With willing hearts we tread
Baptized into our Saviour's death
Blest be the tie that binds
Forbid them not, the Saviour cried
Come, O my soul, in sacred lays
Jesus, these eyes have never seen
My God!  how endless is thy love!
This is the day of rest
All things are thine: no gift have we
God of the universe, to thee
Maker of land and rolling sea
Coming Saviour, now in faith
Each little flow'r that opens
Praise, O praise our God and King!
The spring-tide hour brings leaf and flow'r
Father, we thank thee for the night
Another six days' work is done
Welcome, welcome, day of rest
How sweet upon this sacred day
Come, dearest Lord, and feed thy sheep
One thot' I have, my ample creed
Come, Holy Spirit, calm my mind
I've found the Pearl of greatest price!
Pray'r is the soul's sincere desire
Lord, dismiss us with Thy blessing
Come, let us join our cheerful songs
Praise to Him by whose kind favor
Let thy Spirit, blessed Saviour
God of light and matchless splendor
Christ is ris'n, our Lord and King
High in the heav'ns, eternal God
He reigns!  the Lord, the Saviour reigns!
In the cross of Christ I glory
Once more, my soul, the rising day
How sweet the name of Jesus sounds
Jesus, the very thought of thee
O for a thousand tongues to sing
Before Jehovah's awful throne
Jesus, thou joy of loving hearts!
Sing of Jesus, sing forever
Come, let us sing the song of songs
With rev'rence let the saints appear
How pleasant, how divinely fair
O thou to whom, in ancient time
We may not climb the heav'nly steeps
Our blest Redeemer, ere He breathed
Sweetly the holy hymn
This rite our blest Redeemer gave
While in sweet communion feeding
Thou who on the cross didst make
O give thanks unto the Lord, for He is good
Glory be to the Father
Glory be to the Father
Glory be to the Father
Blessed are the poor in spirit
Our Father, which art in heaven
Praise God, from whom all blessings flow
Be present at our table, Lord
All things come of Thee, O Lord
Around the world the chorus rings
Saved to serve in any station
A year of precious blessings
I love to tell the story
Working O Christ, with thee
Just to trust in the Lord, just to lean on His word
Rescue the perishing
Brightly beams our Father's mercy
Are you Christ's light bearer?
An open Bible for the world!
God bids his people on the earth
Loyalty to the Master, loyalty to the King
Take time to be holy
There are lonely hearts to cherish
I cease to sing of sweet tomorrow
Conquering now and still to conquer
I'm pressing on the upward way
Encamped along the hills of light
Saviour, more than life to me
Does Jesus care when my heart is pain'd
O blessed rest, when we recline
The cross that he gave may be heavy
When we walk with the Lord
Dying with Jesus, by death reckoned mine
Moment by moment, hour by hour
Give me the Bible, star of gladness gleaming
Not a wasted moment in the morning fair
O softly the Spirit is whisp'ring to me
Jesus bids us shine with a pure, clear light
Just lean upon the arms of Jesus
What a fellowship, what a joy divine
God be with you till we meet again
The Lord's our Rock, in him we hide
O safe to the Rock that is higher than I
Under His wings I am safely abiding
O Christian, awake!  'tis the Master's command
Firmly stand for God, in the world's mad strife
Sound the battle cry
Strike!  O strike for vict'ry
Who will volunteer?
Ask not to be excused
My Father is rich in houses and lands
Are you weary, are you heavy hearted?
When peace, like a river attendeth my way
In heav'nly love abiding
As of old when the hosts of Israel
'Tis so sweet to trust in Jesus
Lift him up, 'tis he that bids you
Look upon the golden image
Is there anyone can help us, one who understands our hearts
Look for the beautiful, look for the true
Are you heavy laden and with sorrow tried?
More about Jesus I would know
Father, we come to thee
Saviour, lead me lest I stray
The Lord is my light
The angel of the Lord encampeth
Christ, our mighty Captain, leads against the foe
In the highways and hedges go seek for the lost
Seeking the lost, yes, kindly entreating
Would you win a Saviour's blessing?
Closer to thee, my Father, draw me
Anywhere with Jesus I can safely go
Be with us, Lord, as forth we go
Go ye into all the world
Life is not a cloudless journey
Just a ray of sunshine
Walk in the light the Lord has given
I must have the Saviour with me
What are you doing for Jesus
When the cross seems hard to carry
Throw out the Life-Line across the dark wave
Out upon an angry ocean
On the shore beyond the sea
Far and near the fields are teeming
O where are the reapers that garner in
One more day's work for Jesus
Pray for the erring ones, faith shall reclaim them
The home where changes never come
I am thinking today of that beautiful land
All my class!  not one forgotten
Speak to my soul, dear Jesus
Watch, for the time is short
When softly fades the dying day
Lonely?  no, not lonely
Lifetime is working time, spend no idle days
If any little word of mine
Never be sad or desponding
Hark!  'tis the Shepherd's voice I hear
I have learn'd the wondrous secret
Lord, for tomorrow and its needs
Will your anchor hold in the storm of life
Christian, onward!  Christ is coming
It may not be on the mountain's height
'Tis love that makes us happy
Let us gather up the sunbeams
I wash'd my hands this morning
If your hand's on the plow, hold on, hold on
Master, hast thou work for me?
In the struggle of life there's a conquest to win
Hear the pennies dropping!
Gladly, gladly, toiling for the Master
Give!  said the golden sun
To the work!  to the work!  we are servants of God
Beautiful the little hands
We should be like gardens
If I were a sunbeam
There is beauty all around
Angry words!  oh, let them never
Into the tent where a gypsy boy lay
Sowing in the morning, sowing seeds of kindness
Master, the tempest is raging!
Cold water is the cup that cheers
Look for the way-marks as you journey on
Hear the temp'rance call
He that goeth forth with weeping
The world's glorious harvest is fast drawing on
Man the life-boat!  Man the life-boat!
Father, hear the prayer we offer!
Out from the campfire's red glowing
Now to heav'n our pray'r ascending
Give of your best to the Master
My God, is any hour so sweet
Buy up the opportunity
Blessed Lord, how much I need thee!
Oh, to be "Kept for Jesus!"
I have a Friend so precious
Ready to suffer grief or pain
Precious promise God hath given
Forward, brothers, forward!
God bless our Sabbath school!  Christ superintend it
More diligence give me
Chide mildly the erring
Not now, my child, a little more rough tossing
Let us work for the school with our hearts and our hands
Sweet Sabbath School!  more dear to me
I need Thee every hour
I am passing down the valley that they say is so lone
All the way my Saviour leads me
When morning lights the eastern skies
O sometimes the shadows are deep
Saviour!  Thy dying love
What tho' clouds are hov'ring o'er me
"Call them in," the poor, the wretched
There's a cry from Macedonia  Come and help us
Jesus, tender Shepherd, hear me
The Church has one foundation
I need Thee, precious Jesus
The whole wide world is pleading
Ho!   reapers of life's harvest
For Christ is our endeavor
From Greenland's icy mountains
I could not do without Thee
O Lamb of God! still keep me
Soldiers of the cross, arise!
Stand up! stand up for Jesus!
We plough the fields and scatter
The fields are all white
Lead, kindly Light, amid the encircling gloom
Holy Spirit, faithful Guide
Jesus, lover of my soul
Jesus, lover of my soul
Jesus, lover of my soul
Lord, her watch Thy Church is keeping
We are living, we are dwelling
Lord, thou callest for the workers
"Cast thy bread upon the waters"
Blessed Jesus, meek and lowly
Hark!  the voice of Jesus calling
Pilgrims on!  the day is dawning
What a friend we have in Jesus
Courage, brother!  do not stumble
God the all-terrible!  King who ordainest
Saviour, breathe an evening blessing
Saviour, breathe an evening blessing
Sitting at the feet of Jesus
There is never a day so dreary
I love thee, I love thee
Come, let us anew our journey pursue
Jesus, Saviour, pilot me
"Till He come!"  O let the words
Rock of Ages, cleft for me!
Abide with me!  Fast falls the eventide
Onward, Christian soldiers!
Brightly gleams our banner
Forward!  be our watchword
On our way rejoicing
My Jesus, as thou wilt
Thy way, not mine, O Lord
Saviour!  I follow on
Nearer, my God, to thee
How firm a foundation, ye saints of the Lord
The Lord is my Shepherd, no want shall I know
The Lord is my Shepherd, no want shall I know
Tho' faint, yet pursuing, we go on our way
Still, still with Thee, when purple morning breaketh
Lead Thou me on, and then my feet tho' weary
Christ for the world we sing
O Holy Saviour, Friend unseen
Master, no offering
Answer the call, ye brave men
When storms of life are sweeping
The Lord my pasture shall prepare
Eternal Father!  strong to save
He leadeth me!  O blessed tho't!
Faith of our fathers!  living still
Heir of the kingdom, O why dost thou slumber?
Christian, the morn breaks sweetly o'er thee
A mighty fortress is our God
Rise, my soul, and stretch thy wings
Watchman, blow the gospel trumpet
Words of cheer from the battlefield of life
Cast thy burden on the Lord
We lay us calmly down to sleep
Eternal depth of love divine
God is our refuge and defense
My Maker and my King
O Lord, our heavenly King
I've reach'd the land of corn and wine
I am dwelling on the mountain
The Son of God goes forth to war
O Christian, if the needy poor
Speed on Thy truth, Eternal One
Go forth on wings of faith and pray'r
I will never, never leave thee
Speed thy servants, Saviour, speed them
In the vineyard of our Father
Saints of God, the dawn is bright'ning
Anywhere, dear Saviour
In some way or other, the Lord will provide
Speed away!  speed away, over mountains and sea
Could you wait, could you wait if a brother were lost
Watchman on the walls of Zion
Guide me, O Thou great Jehovah!
Zion stands with walls surrounded
Lo!  He comes, with clouds descending
In the hour of trial
As pants the wearied hart for cooling springs
Revive Thy work, O Lord, Thy mighty arm make bare
If, through unruffled seas
"My times are in thy hand:"
Ye servants of the Lord
Sow in the morn thy seed
How long, O Lord our Saviour
Speak often to each other
The world is very evil
The morning light is breaking
Work, for the night is coming
There is no work too humble
Carry the joyful tidings
O for a faith that will not shrink
How cheering is the Christian's hope
O God our help in ages past
God moves in a mysterious way
Oh, where are the kings and empires now
We bless Thee for Thy peace, O God
Saviour, like a shepherd lead us
God has said, Forever blessed
Always with us, always with us
Meek and lowly, pure and holy
Tossed upon life's raging billow
Watchman, tell me, does the morning
Gracious Father, guard thy children
Long upon the mountains, weary
Mid scenes of confusion and creature complaints
Mid pleasures and palaces though we may roam
Kind words can never die
Out on an ocean all boundless we ride
Sweet hour of pray'r, sweet hour of pray'r
My days are gliding swiftly by
There is a blessed hope
Thus far the Lord has led me on
My spirit on thy care
I ask not, Lord, for less to bear
Children of the heav'nly King
O blest is he who can divine
Sweet the time, exceeding sweet!
Mourn for the thousands slain
Ye Christian heralds!  go, proclaim
Soldiers of Christ, arise
The tempter to my soul hath said
Imposture shrinks from light
This is the day of toil
How blest the sacred tie that binds
Lamp of our feet, Whereby we trace
When waves of trouble round me swell
Happy the home when God is there
So let our lips and lives express
God make my life a little light
Eternal Father, God of love
Come, Holy Spirit, heav'nly Dove
I love the sacred book of God
Amazing grace!  how sweet the sound
How precious is the book divine
I know that my Redeemer lives
Holy Bible!  book divine!
The Lord's my Shepherd, I'll not want
God of love that hearest prayer
Zion, awake!  thy slumber break
How sweet, how heav'nly is the sight
"Go, preach my gospel," saith the Lord
The day is past and gone
Sun of my soul, O Saviour dear!
Lord, in the morning thou shalt hear
My blest Redeemer and my Lord
How sweet the light of Sabbath eve!
Truth, crush'd to earth, shall rise again
Jesus, engrave it on my heart
When pow'r divine, in mortal form
God is the refuge of his saints
He who seeks the truth, and trembles
God of our salvation, hear us
Prince of Peace, control my will
Blest are the pure in heart
We give Thee but thine own
My soul, be on thy guard!
Unshaken as the sacred hills
God's law demands one living faith
Am I a soldier of the cross
O Christ, with each returning morn
Awake my soul!  stretch ev'ry nerve
New ev'ry morning is Thy love
Go, labor on; spend and be spent
Gently think, and gently speak
O Master, let me walk with thee
Lovely this child, asleep in death
One precious boon, O Lord, I seek
Softly fades the twilight ray
